name: Banana Oat Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 cup cake flour (all-purpose is okay but not as tender)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Toast half of the oats in a large skillet over medium heat until they begin to color and become fragrant, about 5 minutes.
3. In a bowl, mash the bananas and mix with honey and vanilla extract. If the banana isn't fully ripe, microwave it for 30 seconds to make it more mushy.
4. Add the toasted oats to the mixture and stir.
5. Mix in the chopped dates, remaining oats, wheat flakes, flour, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt.
6. Form dough into tablespoon-sized balls and sprinkle a little whole wheat flour on each ball to prevent sticking.
7. Place the dough balls onto a parchment-lined or sprayed baking sheet.
8. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until lightly browned. Transfer cookies to a wire rack to cool.
9. Makes approximately 3 dozen cookies.
Storage: Store in an airtight container for several days.
